assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: Report summary table row: '| Test Quality | WARN | Repetitive Test Detection: N/A (no test files), Test Documentation: N/A (no test files), Eval Quality: WARN (eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate; overall 54/57 assertions, 91% pass rate; 2 regressions, 0 pre-existing) |'. Test Quality is WARN, driven by Eval Quality WARN component.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: Report Eval Quality section states: 'Eval result review detected from github-actions[bot] (review ID 40001) via the 3-criteria heuristic: 1. Author is github-actions[bot] 2. Body contains "## Eval Results" marker 3. Body contains "sdlc-workflow/run-evals" footer'. Per-eval metrics table shows pass rates for eval-1 through eval-5. Overall pass rate: 91% (54/57 assertions passed). Review Feedback Processing section confirms: 'Review 40001 from github-actions[bot] was detected as an eval result review via the 3-criteria heuristic and processed for Eval Quality metrics.'

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies comment 50001 as 'code change request' from reviewer-b. Classification reasoning references: (1) directive language ('should'), (2) specific request about Markdown exclusion gap, (3) implementation guidance, (4) review state CHANGES_REQUESTED. No eval metrics appear in the classification reasoning. Separate meta-check confirms: 'This comment is NOT an eval result. It is a normal human review comment from reviewer-b. It does not match the eval result detection criteria.' The classification itself is based on language analysis, not eval processing.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: Test Quality summary row includes 'eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate; overall 54/57 assertions, 91% pass rate'. Detailed Eval Quality section lists two failing assertions: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)' with evidence about missing CONVENTIONS.md lookup, (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path' with evidence about no sub-task created. Both convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation details are present.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md title: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures: convention upgrade eligibility, sub-task creation'. Labels include 'eval-failure'. Description: 'Fix two failing eval-3 assertions in the verify-pr eval suite.' Review Context quotes both failing assertions with their text and evidence. Source line: 'eval-3 from verify-pr eval suite (pass rate: 85%, 11/13 assertions passed, 2 failed)'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md Review Context section (lines 43-59) quotes: Assertion 1: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...' with Evidence: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'. Assertion 2: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with Evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'. Both assertions and evidence are fully quoted.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 39: '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md line 36: '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. Both sub-task descriptions include the Target PR section with the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: Report summary table: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval failure sub-task investigated; classified as skill gap in implement-task phase (convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for suggestions) |'. Detailed section (lines 216-248) shows: Verdict DONE, defect description, universality test (Universal), method-vs-fact test (Method), classification (Skill gap, implement-task phase), phase investigation covering feature description, task description, and implementation analysis, and preventive fix recommendation. The verdict is DONE, not N/A.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are verification artifacts: report.md (verification report), criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria analysis), review-50001.md and review-body-40002.md (review classifications), subtask-1.md and subtask-2.md (sub-task descriptions). All files are in /tmp/verify-pr-eval-baseline/eval-6/outputs/. No code files (source code, configuration, or implementation files) were modified.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: Scope Containment (report lines 28-35): PASS, compares PR files (style-conventions.md, SKILL.md) against task files, finds 0 out-of-scope and 0 unimplemented -- no reference to eval results. Sensitive Patterns (report lines 65-73): PASS, scans 44 added lines across 2 Markdown files for passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, etc. -- no reference to eval results. Acceptance Criteria (report lines 89-125): PASS, 7/7 criteria verified with code-level evidence from the diff (section names, verdict definitions, output format changes, mapping rows) -- each criterion references diff content, not eval results.
